title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body: If we touch too quickly after file creation, it would not noticably update the timestamp.

step_plan.summary: Make diff command test deterministic on macOS HFS+ by adding a darwin-only 1s sleep before touching a file so the filesystem timestamp granularity does not make the touch a no-op.

steps.operation: Add darwin-only sleep before touching file to ensure timestamp changes
steps.reason: On macOS HFS+ the filesystem timestamp granularity is 1s; touching a file immediately after creation can leave mtime/ctime unchanged and make the test flaky. Inserting a conditional time.sleep(1) when running on Darwin ensures the touch updates the timestamp and the test reliably detects the change.
steps.side_effects: Increases test runtime on macOS by ~1s for this test only; no production API changes. Reduces flakiness of this test on Darwin. Ensure that the module still imports time and is_darwin; if not present, adding an import will be necessary (this would be a small test-only change). Other tests should be unaffected, but CI run time on macOS runners will be slightly longer.
